title: Yorkies dance to the rhythm of their hearts
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - York High's Greatest Dancer competition and show bedazzled a packed audience in the school hall last Thursday, 22 May.


> The learners showcased their skills in jazz, modern dance and hip-hop, delivering performances that rivalled professional dancers.


Their family and friends had the chance to see all their exam work on stage - hours of blood, sweat and tears.

The energy was infectious, and every move showed their talent, passion and dedication.

PHOTO GALLERY - York's Greatest Dancer competition: A showcase of talent and passion

From intricate jazz routines to dynamic hip-hop beats and expressive modern dance, the show was a vibrant celebration of creativity and skill. What a performance!

Accolades

The top performers were as follows: The winner of the York's Greatest Dancer Award was Sophia Morley.

The runner-up was Olivia Goussard and in third place was Tyla Dafel.

Khloë Barnard received the honours for the best performance, and Isabella Botes won the amateur division.

The organisers would like to thank all the sponsors for their support.

Grade 10s: Inathi Somana, Tylah Hector and Amahle Luhlongwane.

Zoë Reid and Tamryn Pieterse in Ghost Dances.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
